title: Process Engineer
description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

ACE WATER PTE. LTD. is a rapidly expanding water treatment company specializing in consultancy and EPC turnkey services for water treatment, reclamation, and desalination plants in Singapore and the Asia Pacific region. We develop innovative, robust, and cost-competitive solutions based on extensive field experience and in-depth understanding of both conventional and state-of-the-art technologies for municipal and industrial water and wastewater challenges. We seek responsible, passionate, and collaborative team players to join us.

Job Summary

You will design and deliver detailed piping arrangements and equipment layouts for water treatment plants and modular skids, driving project execution from technical specification through onsite support to as-built documentation. Your expertise will ensure optimal component selection and coordination with multidisciplinary teams to achieve efficient, compliant, and cost-effective plant solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Design detailed piping arrangements for water treatment plants, producing isometric drawings, piping support drawings, and bills of materials (BOM) to ensure constructability and accuracy
  • Prepare and design equipment layouts for complete plants and modular skids, including Process Flow Diagrams (PFD), Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (PID), pump and equipment outline drawings, and Electrical, Instrumentation, and Control Automation (EICA) drawings to support project planning and execution
  • Calculate pressure drops and prepare piping specifications, technical datasheets, and purchase documentation to facilitate accurate procurement of piping components
  • Size and select pumps, equipment, piping, and fittings to meet system performance and reliability requirements
  • Coordinate and interface effectively with process, instrumentation, civil, and equipment suppliers to align design and project deliverables
  • Evaluate and clarify vendor quotations to ensure compliance with technical and commercial requirements
  • Provide onsite technical support during project execution to resolve issues and ensure design intent is maintained
  • Prepare and update as-built drawings to reflect final installation and support project close-out
  • Execute other ad-hoc tasks as assigned to support project and team objectives

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Diploma or Degree in Mechanical Engineering or a relevant field
  • Proficiency in Revit, AutoCAD Plant 3D, and isometric drawing preparation
  • Approximately 3 years of relevant experience in the water treatment or related industry
  • Ability to collaborate effectively across all workforce levels
  • Self-motivated with strong teamwork orientation
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills
